scope_summary: Protects and preserves Western Australia's rural land for its economic, natural resource, food production, environmental and landscape values. Applies to rural-zoned land, rural living areas and rural land uses state-wide (with exemptions including BRM extraction in Perth/Peel and Mining Act matters), and guides scheme-making, zoning proposals, subdivision and development on rural land, including criteria for rural living precincts, tree farming, animal premises, intensive agriculture and buffers to sensitive land uses. Replaces SPP 2.5 Land Use Planning in Rural Areas (2013) and repeals SPP 4.3 Poultry Farms (1998); supersedes SPP 2.4 (2000) for the shires of Gingin, Chittering, Northam and Toodyay.

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: rural living zones (rural living, rural retreat, rural residential, special rural, rural smallholdings, rural conservation, landscape protection)
rural residential (special rural) zone land parcels
rural smallholdings (rural retreat) zone land parcels
rural living lots of 4 ha or less require reticulated water supply; above 4 ha WAPC may consider fit-for-purpose domestic potable supply incl. firefighting water
rural enterprise (rural industry/composite) zone lots combining light industry and ancillary dwelling
